title: Inflated GDP?, Google earnings, How the media lost trust, Rogan/Trump search controversy, Election!
description:
(0:00) Bestie intros!

(5:40) US Real GDP growth comes in at 2.8%, but there are underlying issues

(29:16) Google earnings: YouTube and Cloud post huge quarters, would they have survived outside of Google?

(36:24) Sacks's idea to auction off public spectrum licenses of major broadcast networks

(42:17) How the media became one of the least trusted institutions in the US

(54:26) Why Joe Rogan's interview with Trump was not appearing in YouTube search results

(1:08:54) Final pre-election segment: how it's tracking, election integrity, voter fraud stats

Get notified for the Election Night Livestream:

analysis.summary: The episode covers various topics including the US Real GDP growth, Google earnings, the decline of trust in the media, and the upcoming election. The hosts discuss the underlying issues with the GDP growth and the impressive performance of YouTube and Cloud. They also delve into the reasons behind the media's loss of trust and the controversy surrounding Joe Rogan's interview with Trump on YouTube.

takeaways.text: US Real GDP growth is 2.8%, but has underlying issues
YouTube and Cloud had huge quarters
Media is one of the least trusted institutions in the US
Joe Rogan's interview with Trump was not appearing in YouTube search results
Election integrity and voter fraud are concerns
Google's cost savings efforts are ongoing
Auctioning off public spectrum licenses could be beneficial

sentiment.notes: The hosts express concern over the underlying issues with the GDP growth and the decline of trust in the media. They also discuss the controversy surrounding Joe Rogan's interview with Trump, which adds to the cautious tone.

notable_moments.description: Discussion on election integrity and voter fraud
